message:
fix(fcp-raptorq): use intermediate symbol count l for decoder state initialization

Fixes a critical sizing bug in InactivationDecoder where active_cols
and solved were initialized using k (source symbol count) instead of l
(total intermediate symbol count including LDPC/HDPC overhead).  Using
k truncated the Gaussian elimination column set, producing incorrect
results when intermediate symbols > source symbols.

New test decode_wavefront_matches_sequential_for_intermediate_symbol_recovery
creates a systematic encoding with k=24 symbols, generates repair
symbols at 25% ratio, drops every 7th symbol, then verifies that
decode_wavefront (parallel) and decode (sequential) produce identical
source and intermediate output — directly exercising the l vs k fix.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
